title: ATTITUDES TOWARDS ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
content_html:
Attitudes towards Artificial Intelligence

A.

Artificial intelligence (AI) can already predict the future. Police forces are using it to map when and where crime is likely to occur. Doctors can use it to predict when a patient is most likely to have a heart attack or stroke. Researchers are even trying to give AI imagination so it can plan for unexpected consequences.

Many decisions in our lives require a good forecast, and AI is almost always better at forecasting than we are. Yet for all these technological advances, we still seem to deeply lack confidence in AI predictions. Recent cases show that people don’t like relying on AI and prefer to trust human experts, even if these experts are wrong.

If we want AI to really benefit people, we need to find a way to get people to trust it. To do that, we need to understand why people are so reluctant to trust AI in the first place.

B.

Take the case of Watson for Oncology, one of technology giant IBM’s supercomputer programs. Their attempt to promote this program to cancer doctors was a PR disaster. The AI promised to deliver top-quality recommendations on the treatment of 12 cancers that accounted for 80% of the world’s cases. But when doctors first interacted with Watson, they found themselves in a rather difficult situation. On the one hand, if Watson provided guidance about a treatment that coincided with their own opinions, physicians did not see much point in Watson’s recommendations. The supercomputer was simply telling them what they already knew, and these recommendations did not change the actual treatment.

On the other hand, if Watson generated a recommendation that contradicted the experts’ opinion, doctors would typically conclude that Watson wasn’t competent. And the machine wouldn’t be able to explain why its treatment was plausible because its machine-learning algorithms were simply too complex to be fully understood by humans. Consequently, this has caused even more suspicion and disbelief, leading many doctors to ignore the seemingly outlandish AI recommendations and stick to their own expertise.

C.

This is just one example of people’s lack of confidence in AI and their reluctance to accept what AI has to offer. Trust in other people is often based on our understanding of how others think and having experience of their reliability. This helps create a psychological feeling of safety. AI, on the other hand, is still fairly new and unfamiliar to most people. Even if it can be technically explained (and that’s not always the case), AI’s decision-making process is usually too difficult for most people to comprehend. And interacting with something we don’t understand can cause anxiety and give us a sense that we’re losing control.

Many people are also simply not familiar with many instances of AI actually working, because it often happens in the background. Instead, they are acutely aware of instances where AI goes wrong. Embarrassing AI failures receive a disproportionate amount of media attention, emphasising the message that we cannot rely on technology. Machine learning is not foolproof, in part because the humans who design it aren’t.

D.

Feelings about AI run deep. In a recent experiment, people from a range of backgrounds were given various sci-fi films about AI to watch and then asked questions about automation in everyday life. It was found that, regardless of whether the film they watched depicted AI in a positive or negative light, simply watching a cinematic vision of our technological future polarized the participants’ attitudes. Optimists became more extreme in their enthusiasm for AI and skeptics became even more guarded.

This suggests people use relevant evidence about AI in a biased manner to support their existing attitudes, a deep-rooted human tendency known as “confirmation bias”. As AI is represented more and more in media and entertainment, it could lead to a society split between those who benefit from AI and those who reject it. More pertinently, refusing to accept the advantages offered by AI could place a large group of people at a serious disadvantage.

E.

Fortunately, we already have some ideas about how to improve trust in AI. Simply having previous experience with AI can significantly improve people’s opinions about the technology, as was found in the study mentioned above. Evidence also suggests the more you use other technologies such as the internet, the more you trust them.

Another solution may be to reveal more about the algorithms which AI uses and the purposes they serve. Several high-profile social media companies and online marketplaces already release transparency reports about government requests and surveillance disclosures. A similar practice for AI could help people have a better understanding of the way algorithmic decisions are made.

F.

Research suggests that allowing people some control over AI decision-making could also improve trust and enable AI to learn from human experience. For example, one study showed that when people were allowed the freedom to slightly modify an algorithm, they felt more satisfied with its decisions, more likely to believe it was superior and more likely to use it in the future.

We don’t need to understand the intricate inner workings of AI systems, but if people are given a degree of responsibility for how they are implemented, they will be more willing to accept AI into their lives.

options: i=An increasing divergence of attitudes towards AI
ii=Reasons why we have more faith in human judgement than in AI
iii=The superiority of AI projections over those made by humans
iv=The process by which AI can help us make good decisions
v=The advantages of involving users in AI processes
vi=Widespread distrust of an AI innovation
vii=Encouraging openness about how AI functions
viii=A surprisingly successful AI application
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 27–32

Reading Passage has paragraphs A–F. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.

Write the correct number, i–viii, in boxes 27–32 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
AI predict the future
better at forecasting than humans
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn A: “AI is almost always better at forecasting than we are.”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn nhấn mạnh AI dự đoán tốt hơn con người.
“better at forecasting” = superiority of AI projections.
=> Đáp án: iii. The superiority of AI projections over those made by humans

headings:
An increasing divergence of attitudes towards Al
Reasons why we have more faith in human judgement than in Al
The superiority of AI projections over those made by humans
The process by which Al can help us make good decisions
The advantages of involving users in Al processes
Widespread distrust of an AI innovation
Encouraging openness about how Al functions
A surprisingly successful Al application

- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**

questions.questionHtml:
List of Headings

  1. An increasing divergence of attitudes towards Al
  2. Reasons why we have more faith in human judgement than in Al
  3. The superiority of AI projections over those made by humans
  4. The process by which Al can help us make good decisions
  5. The advantages of involving users in Al processes
  6. Widespread distrust of an AI innovation
  7. Encouraging openness about how Al functions
  8. A surprisingly successful Al application

27_______  Paragraph A

28_______  Paragraph B

29_______  Paragraph C

30_______  Paragraph D

31_______  Paragraph E

32_______  Paragraph F

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Watson for Oncology
suspicion and disbelief
doctors ignore AI
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn B:
“this has caused even more suspicion and disbelief”
“doctors ignore the AI recommendations”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn nói về việc bác sĩ không tin tưởng AI Watson.
“suspicion and disbelief” = widespread distrust.
=> Đáp án: vi. Widespread distrust of an AI innovation

Bước 1: Keyword
trust human judgement
AI unfamiliar
losing control
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn C:
“Trust in other people is often based on…”
“AI is still fairly new and unfamiliar”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn giải thích vì sao con người tin người hơn AI.
Vì AI khó hiểu, mới lạ, gây cảm giác mất kiểm soát.
=> Đáp án: ii. Reasons why we have more faith in human judgement than in AI

Bước 1: Keyword
optimists became more extreme
sceptics became more guarded
polarised attitudes
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn D:
“polarised the participants’ attitudes”
Bước 3: Phân tích
AI khiến thái độ con người phân hóa mạnh hơn.
Người thích AI càng thích, người nghi ngờ càng nghi ngờ.
=> Đáp án: i. An increasing divergence of attitudes towards AI

Bước 1: Keyword
reveal more about algorithms
transparency reports
understanding decisions
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn E:
“reveal more about the algorithms”
“transparency reports”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn nói cần minh bạch hơn về cách AI hoạt động.
“transparency” = openness.
=> Đáp án: vii. Encouraging openness about how AI functions

Bước 1: Keyword
allow people control
modify an algorithm
more satisfied
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn F:
“allowing people some control”
“modify an algorithm”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Khi người dùng tham gia vào AI process, họ tin AI hơn.
Đoạn tập trung vào lợi ích của việc involve users.
=> Đáp án: v. The advantages of involving users in AI processes

options: A. providing a solution to a concern
B. justifying an opinion about an issue
C. highlighting the existence of a problem
D. explaining the reasons for a phenomenon
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Section A
writer doing
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn A:
“people don’t like relying on AI”
“prefer to trust human experts”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Tác giả đang nêu ra một vấn đề: con người thiếu niềm tin vào AI.
Không phải đưa giải pháp hay giải thích nguyên nhân chi tiết.
=> Đáp án: C. highlighting the existence of a problem

- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**

questions.question_text: What is the writer doing in Section A?

options: A. They are afraid it will replace humans in decision-making jobs.
B. Its complexity makes them feel that they are at a disadvantage.
C. They would rather wait for the technology to be tested over a period of time.
D. Misunderstandings about how it works make it seem more challenging than it is.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
reluctant to accept AI
Section C
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn C:
“AI’s decision-making process is usually too difficult…”
“give us a sense that we’re losing control”
Bước 3: Phân tích
AI quá phức tạp khiến con người cảm thấy bất lợi/mất kiểm soát.
Khớp với ý “at a disadvantage”.
=> Đáp án: B
questions.question_text: According to Section C, why might some people be reluctant to accept AI?

options: A. It leads the public to be mistrustful of AI.
B. It devotes an excessive amount of attention to AI.
C. Its reports of incidents involving AI are often inaccurate.
D. It gives the impression that AI failures are due to designer error.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
media
Section C
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn C: “AI failures receive a disproportionate amount of media attention”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Truyền thông tập trung nhiều vào thất bại của AI.
Điều này khiến công chúng mất niềm tin.
=> Đáp án: A
questions.question_text: What does the writer say about the media in Section C of the text?

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 36–40

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 36–40 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
sci-fi films
change opinions
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn D: “polarised the participants’ attitudes”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Người xem không đổi hẳn quan điểm.
Người thích AI càng thích hơn, người nghi ngờ càng nghi ngờ hơn.
Statement nói “change opinions” theo nghĩa đổi ý → không đúng.
=> Đáp án: NO

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: Subjective depictions of AI in sci-fi films make people change their opinions about automation.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
portrayals
more positive
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn D
Bước 3: Phân tích
Passage chỉ nói AI xuất hiện ngày càng nhiều trong media.
Không nói portrayal sẽ tích cực hơn.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Portrayals of AI in media and entertainment are likely to become more positive.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
rejection of AI
negative effect
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn D: “could place a large group of people at a serious disadvantage”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Từ chối AI có thể khiến nhiều người gặp bất lợi nghiêm trọng.
“serious disadvantage” = negative effect.
=> Đáp án: YES
questions.question_text: Rejection of the possibilities of AI may have a negative effect on many people's lives.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
familiarity with AI
little impact
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn E: “previous experience with AI can significantly improve people’s opinions”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Passage nói familiarity có ảnh hưởng lớn.
Statement nói “very little impact”.
Trái nghĩa.
=> Đáp án: NO
questions.question_text: Familiarity with AI has very little impact on people's attitudes to the technology.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
modify
more satisfied
more likely to use
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn F:
“modify an algorithm”
“more satisfied”
“more likely to use it in the future”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Khi người dùng được chỉnh AI, họ hài lòng và chấp nhận hơn.
“gain consumer approval” = được người dùng chấp nhận.
=> Đáp án: YES
questions.question_text: AI applications which users are able to modify are more likely to gain consumer approval.
